scope: national popular vote + abstentions for US presidential elections 2008-2024
purpose: Evaluation targets only. NOT for tuning the model.
primary_denominator: VEP (Voting-Eligible Population) per US Elections Project / UF Election Lab
fallback_denominator: Census CPS CVAP if VEP source unavailable for a year
convention_no_invented_numbers: Cells where exact value cannot be locally verified are null with needs_source: true.
phase_2_1_update_summary: VEP turnout rates verified for all 5 years from UF Election Lab CSV (US_VEP_Turnout_Rates_v1.2.csv). 2024 VEP, total ballots, and per-state data fully verified from Turnout_2024G_v0.4.csv. FEC PDFs for all 5 years cached locally under data/backtest-sources/fec/ (untracked).
source_hierarchy: FEC official presidential vote totals (cached PDFs)
US Elections Project / UF Election Lab VEP (cached CSVs)
Census CPS Voting and Registration Supplement (secondary)
CES/CCES (primary respondent-level survey microdata, separate file)

cached_source_files.note: All cached locally under data/backtest-sources/ — NOT committed to repo per global rules.

ufl_methodology_caveat: UF's TURNOUT_RATE_PRES is computed as ballots-counted / VEP. For 2024 this is 64.30% (156,766,239 / 243,803,423). The presidential-vote-only turnout rate, computed as FEC presidential votes / VEP, is 63.67% (155,238,302 / 243,803,423). The 1.5M difference is downballot-only voters / presidential undervotes. The backtest comparison uses presidential-vote-only since the simulator predicts presidential vote choice.

2008.d_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against cached PDF
2008.r_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against cached PDF
2008.vep_source: US Elections Project / UF Election Lab
2008.vep_count_acquisition_note: UF 1948-2022 CSV provides rate only, not VEP count. Pull McDonald's per-year archive (Turnout_2008G_*.csv) to fill.
2008.turnout_vep_verification: UF Election Lab CSV verified — US_VEP_Turnout_Rates_v1.2.csv row YEAR=2008
2008.notes: High-turnout cycle. D wins both popular vote and EC. Other includes Nader, Barr, Baldwin (CON), McKinney (GRE).

2016.vep_source: US Elections Project / UF Election Lab

2016.notes: D wins popular vote, R wins EC. CCES 2016 microdata available locally for backtest.

2020.r_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against cached PDF
2020.total_presidential_votes_verification: approximate (158.4 million ballots) per UF Election Lab snippet; verify exact via cached PDF
2020.vep_source: US Elections Project / UF Election Lab
2020.turnout_vep_verification: UF Election Lab CSV verified — US_VEP_Turnout_Rates_v1.2.csv row YEAR=2020. RESOLVES prior 66.1% vs 66.6% discrepancy — canonical is 0.6638 (66.38%).
2020.notes: Highest VEP turnout in over a century. UF rate now reconciled at 66.38%.

2024.d_votes_verification: FEC PDF directly verified — page 3 of Official 2024 Presidential General Election Results

other_votes_breakdown_partial.note: Partial breakdown from FEC PDF; full per-candidate splits available across pages 2-6 of cached PDF

2024.vep_source: US Elections Project / UF Election Lab
2024.vep_count_verification: UF Election Lab CSV verified — Turnout_2024G_v0.4.csv 'United States' row
2024.turnout_uf_ballots_based_verification: UF Election Lab CSV verified — VEP_TURNOUT_RATE column
2024.turnout_presidential_vep_verification: derived = 155,238,302 / 243,803,423 — THIS is the rate to use for the backtest since predictions are on FEC presidential votes
2024.notes: First R popular-vote win since 2004. Notable third-party: Kennedy 756,393, De La Cruz 166,175, West 82,644, Stein, Oliver, etc.

verified_vs_needs_source_summary_post_ph.highest_confidence_year: 2024 (FEC PDF + UF CSV both directly verified for D, R, Other, Total, VEP, all derived metrics)

All 5 FEC PDFs cached locally for follow-up verification
verified_vs_needs_source_summary_post_ph.remaining_largest_gap: VEP for 2008/2012/2016/2020 — UF 1948-2022 CSV provides rates only, not VEP counts. Pull from McDonald's per-year archive next.

comparison_anchors_2024_runnable.note: 2024 is fully runnable as a backtest comparison anchor — all 9 evaluation metrics computable from these verified inputs.
